item.title: Man jailed for running over and seriously injuring SA Police officer
item.description: A man, who was on the run from authorities in Victoria, has been jailed for eight years after mowing down a police officer in South Australia.

item.title: Iran says ticket allocation for World Cup withdrawn days before tournament
item.description: Iran's football federation says the 8 per cent quota of tickets allocated to Iran for the FIFA World Cup has been revoked, meaning "there is no possibility to distribute tickets to fans".

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Disney on Ice spoiled the sport's finals plans, so how does it secure better venues?
item.description: The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new standard with a record crowd at one of the biggest stadiums in the country. But in 2026, the sport has been forced to retreat to smaller finals venues due to a scheduling issue with Disney on Ice.

item.title: Confidence assured in Macquarie Point Stadium delivery at meeting of AFL presidents
item.description: Despite suggestions that potential delays and cost blowouts of Tasmania's new stadium may be on the minds of AFL heads, the topic was barely mentioned at the presidents' annual meeting.

item.title: Priscilla Hon calls out prize money disparity at Libéma Open tennis tournament
item.description: Priscilla Hon draws attention to the difference between the men's and women's winners' prize money at the Libéma Open in The Netherlands.

item.title: Encrypted apps including Signal and Discord used by criminals to lure teens with intellectual disabilities into crime
item.description: Criminal syndicates are targeting vulnerable children with no criminal history, low IQ and intellectual disabilities to carry out their dirty work through encrypted apps.

item.title: Teens recruited by crime syndicates for arson attacks
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Palestinians 'trapped' between Israeli forces, settlers and Hamas, UN report finds
item.description: A UN report finds that Palestinians are being subjected to "mass atrocities" by Israeli forces and being "violently repressed and controlled by the very faction that claims to govern them".

item.title: Tens of thousands displaced after magnitude-7.8 earthquake in Philippines
item.description: Rescuers are combing through collapsed buildings in the southern Philippines after a powerful earthquake devastated coastal communities.

item.title: Sienna Toohey makes Commonwealth Games swimming team after move to Canberra
item.description: Seventeen-year-old Sienna Toohey says qualifying for the Commonwealth Games makes moving away from friends and family to Canberra worth it.

item.title: Northern Ireland police appeal for calm after asylum seeker arrested over Belfast stabbing
item.description: Protesters hold anti-immigration demonstrations in Northern Ireland and England after a Sudanese man is arrested over a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

item.title: Eight killed in south Lebanon strike as Israel warns entire city to evacuate
item.description: For the first time, Israel's military has warned the entire city of Tyre to evacuate, causing thousands of people to flee.

item.title: Trump vows to respond after Iran shoots down helicopter patrolling Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump accuses Iran of shooting down a US Apache helicopter that was patrolling the Strait of Hormuz, vowing to retaliate.

item.title: Travel quiz, June 3: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off on a trip
item.description: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

item.title: How Rolf Harris hid in plain sight
item.description: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

item.title: Fuel shock spurs business uptake of green equipment including batteries and EVs
item.description: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

item.title: ABC is looking for storytellers for International Day of People with Disability
item.description: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: When does it start, who are the favourites, what time do the Socceroos play?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

item.title: Some countries are protecting a child's right to play — not Australia
item.description: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: play time.

item.title: Melbourne councils say state government task force to crack down on illegal dumping is ineffective
item.description: Councils across Melbourne's suburbs are struggling to combat the rise of illegal dumping, despite over $21 million of funding from the Victorian state government.

item.title: Palestinian father vows to fight for justice for seven-month-old baby killed by Israeli troops
item.description: A Palestinian father promises to fight for justice for his seven-month-old son, who was shot dead by an Israeli soldier in the West Bank city of Hebron.

item.title: Bus overcrowding prompts push for more Brisbane transit lanes
item.description: Buses are Queensland's most popular form of public transport, but advocates say a lack of investment means they are often unreliable and overcrowded.

item.title: Gary Ablett Jr, John Worsfold among Australian Football Hall of Fame inductees
item.description: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

item.title: Xi Jinping's North Korea visit is unusual, and shows his need to court Kim Jong Un
item.description: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

item.title: Call for security boost at shopping centres to clamp down on knife risk
item.description: A retail worker at Sydney's Broadway Shopping Centre says she was left scared after a man told her he had a knife.

item.title: Australia records first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years as hosts win by 86 runs
item.description: Bangladesh posts just its second victory over Australia in 23 ODIs, recording an 86-run win in the opening match of the series in Dhaka.

item.title: Serena Williams makes winning return to court at Queen's in doubles match
item.description: Serena Williams steals the show at Queen's Club, as the American icon marks her eagerly awaited return to competitive action with a doubles victory nearly four years after her last match.

item.title: Mould, overcrowding and housing delays affecting families in Wilcannia
item.description: Delays in maintenance repairs, including mould removal, are forcing families to sleep on mattresses in living rooms, adding to overcrowding pressures in remote homes.

item.title: Chinese-launched rocket lights up Australian skies as it heads for orbit
item.description: Some skywatchers describe a silvery or blue-tinted ball of gas that gradually turned red and disappeared.

item.title: NSW and federal government tension over hospital bed backlog — as it happened
item.description: NSW Health Minister Ryan Park has aired his frustrations over the issue of people stuck in hospital beds waiting for a spot in aged care or an NDIS placement.

item.title: NASA unveils Artemis III astronauts to test technology for future moon landing
item.description: NASA has revealed the crew for its Artemis III mission, the next step in the US space agency's plan to eventually land astronauts on the moon.

item.title: Sugarcane industry worries about future as Cairns's urban growth encroaches
item.description: Sugarcane growers near Cairns say urban expansion is reducing farmland and putting the region's sugar industry at risk.

item.title: Trump threatens new US strikes, saying Iran's 'taken too long' to make deal - as it happened
item.description: Donald Trump has threatened to strike Iran's power plants and bridges, saying they 'have to pay the price' for taking too long to negotiate.

item.title: Nick Kyrgios wins opening match in Stuttgart after five-month absence from ATP Tour
item.description: Nick Kyrgios makes a successful return to the court, claiming only his second singles victory since 2022.

item.title: Gippsland croquet player attempts to break world record at 101 years old
item.description: Gippsland's May Beveridge still plays croquet every week. The 101-year-old may soon be crowned the world's oldest female competitive player.

item.title: Emergency clean-up ordered at Biloela's Essential Spill
item.description: Drums and containers of chemical and hazardous materials have been leaking at an abandoned central Queensland premises, flowing into stormwater drains.

item.title: Cameron McEvoy says 50m world record just the beginning, Australian Swimming Trials first chance to go faster
item.description: When Cameron McEvoy broke the long-standing 50m freestyle world record in March, he was at great pains to note that he believes he can go even faster. The Australian Swimming Trials offers the first chance to do so.

item.title: Elon Musk's xAI hit with class action over data centre power plants causing 'inescapable' noise
item.description: US residents say a plant powering data centres has caused them emotional distress and reduced the value of their properties.

item.title: Man charged with murder over stabbing of Brandon Yates in Wollongong
item.description: Police have charged a man and say they expect more arrests in relation to the stabbing of Brandon Yates, who was found fatally injured in an apartment in Wollongong in 2025.

item.title: Mystery blue streak spotted over southern Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Omar Artan returns home after being denied entry to United States ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: Omar Artan thanks FIFA for its support after he is barred from entering the United States, ending his dream of refereeing at his first World Cup.

item.title: China's Zhuque-2E Y6 rocket launches two new satellites into orbit
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aussie magic in Oakland drives Socceroos at FIFA World Cup
item.description: Socceroos captain Mat Ryan is a long way from home, but he has found an unexpected connection in Oakland.

item.title: Mark Latham to pay MP Alex Greenwich $140k as appeal over homophobic tweet judgement fails
item.description: A Federal Court judge has dismissed an appeal against a judgement which ordered New South Wales MP Mark Latham to pay $140,000 to independent MP Alex Greenwich over a homophobic tweet in 2023.

item.title: Court dismisses appeal to increase jail sentence for killer driver Christopher Dillon Joannidis
item.description: The Court of Appeal has dismissed an appeal to increase the jail term of Christopher Dillon Joannidis, who killed five people in a horrific 2023 crash in Northern Victoria.

item.title: Domestic violence escalated during Hayley's pregnancy — a time when women are at greater risk
item.description: Pregnancy is a time of greater autonomy and self-awareness for women. Because domestic violence is about power and control, a pregnancy can challenge that for a person who is using abuse.

item.title: Former FIFA official says profit being prioritised over fans at World Cup
item.description: A former FIFA official says the upcoming World Cup has been tainted by corporate greed as two US states investigate ticket pricing and practices.

item.title: Anthony Albanese 'very worried' about human and global economic impacts of Iran war escalation
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says he is "very worried" about fresh US strikes on Iran and has highlighted the difficulties for global markets responding to US President Donald Trump's repeated peace deal announcements.

item.title: Blues star Brian To'o on the verge of breaking 35-year-old Origin try-scoring record
item.description: Brian To'o is one of the best New South Wales Origin players in recent years and now history beckons for the Blues winger in Game II at the MCG.

item.title: Neale Daniher's family and friends, AFL identities gather at MCG for MND campaigner's state funeral
item.description: Family, friends, members of the AFL community and dignitaries attend the service to remember the AFL legend, MND campaigner and former Australia of the Year.

item.title: Cyber attack shuts down two Mackay Sugar mills
item.description: Cane farmers in the Mackay region were notified early on Wednesday to stop harvesting, after a cyber incident brought sugar milling at two north Queensland mills to a halt.

item.title: Woolworths to offshore hundreds of corporate jobs
item.description: Supermarket giant Woolworths will begin consultations with potentially affected staff today after announcing plans to shift some operations offshore.

item.title: Jeremy Rockliff admits failings over Madeleine Ogilvie allegations of misleading parliament
item.description: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff says he "dropped the ball" regarding the allegations against a former minister, likening it to a "pile of crap" he has to clean up from "time to time".

item.title: NDIS cuts could create unsafe environments and domestic violence risks, inquiry warned
item.description: Planned cuts to NDIS community participation budgets could leave participants in unsafe environments, including increasing their risk of domestic violence, advocates have warned.

item.title: Victorian coroner recommends mental fitness requirement for gun owners
item.description: The coroner made the recommendation as part of an inquiry into the death of a gun owner who failed to self-report an extensive history of mental health and substance use issues when renewing his licence.

item.title: Bear tranquilised after roaming suburb in Japan
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Japan's bear attacks are breaking records and spilling into big cities
item.description: Bear encounters are rising in Japan, and the problem is not confined to the wilderness.

item.title: Traveller dies after contracting Murray Valley Encephalitis in WA
item.description: One person has died after contracting Murray Valley Encephalitis in Western Australia's Kimberley region.

item.title: CFMEU pressured building and construction commission to 'take action' against certain contractors, inquiry hears
item.description: Former Queensland Building and Construction Commission (QBCC) licencing manager Graham Easterby told the inquiry the regulator was pressured to target certain contractors by the CFMEU.

item.title: José Carreras to play Gabba show with Robbie Williams
item.description: Revered opera star José Carreras will lead a line-up featuring everyone from Robbie Williams to Sheppard at the Gabba's first concert in eight years.

item.title: High Court decision on NZYQ could lead to tens of millions of dollars worth of immigration claims
item.description: There are warnings a High Court ruling allowing a man to sue for false imprisonment over his indefinite immigration detention will lead to more claims running into the tens of millions of dollars.

item.title: Inpex seeks urgent orders to stop Darwin strike as experts fear impact on gas market could be significant
item.description: Oil and gas giant Inpex has launched proceedings with the Fair Work Commission seeking urgent orders to stop strike action at its Ichthys LNG facility in Darwin.

item.title: Beekeepers call for biopesticide known to target varroa mite to come to Australia
item.description: A new biopesticide could help battle growing resistance to varroa mite treatments in Australia.

item.title: Traditional owners' $2 million loss as Nathan River Resources collapses 'undermines' promises
item.description: Nathan River Resources went into administration and owes about $360 million to creditors including the NT government and traditional owners.

item.title: Nant whisky founder Keith Batt to enter months-long trial in the Supreme Court of Tasmania
item.description: Tasmanian whisky distillery owner Keith Batt is charged with more than 700 criminal offences, ranging from fraud, to stealing, to dishonestly obtaining a financial advantage.

item.title: Police release body-worn footage of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham's arrest, lay fresh charges
item.description: Northern Territory Police have released dramatic body-worn footage of the arrest of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham after the 44-year-old jumped a court dock last week, sparking a five-day manhunt.

item.title: Mark Schwarzer backs Socceroos to make FIFA World Cup impression in US
item.description: Maligned, underestimated and overlooked — and that is just the way we like it according to the Socceroos' most capped player.

item.title: Sea drone comes to rescue after US helicopter downed near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A look at the US Corsair sea drone used in a first of its kind mission to rescue two helicopter crew shot down near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Why Bunnings's plan to move 10km from Bunbury has become a 'red hot' political issue
item.description: A plan by hardware giant Bunnings move its Bunbury store 10km to a neighbouring shire has angered and concerned residents and businesses.

item.title: Skipper recounts mission to rescue dog swept out to sea
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Military officers behind acid attack on Indonesian activist sentenced to jail
item.description: Four military intelligence officers are sentenced to up to three years in jail, after they threw sulphuric acid over a human rights activist in a brazen attack in Central Jakarta.

item.title: TGA cracks down on unregulated peptides, says increased imports are posing a risk to consumer safety
item.description: The Therapeutic Goods Administration has launched a crackdown on unregulated peptides, saying increased illegal imports were posing a risk to consumers.

item.title: Canberrans looking for cost-of-living relief in ACT budget might be left wanting
item.description: During practice at this Canberra football club, players discuss the ways they are saving money. But if they were looking for cost-of-living relief in today's ACT budget, they might not have found what they were looking for.

item.title: Media outlets ask Supreme Court judge to overturn magistrate’s suppression order in Cairns extortion case
item.description: A non-publication order suppressing the identity of a high-profile Queensland man named in a Cairns extortion case will be tested in the Supreme Court.

item.title: Former NT Minister claims child neglect is being 'hushed up' in Northern Territory Indigenous communities
item.description: A review has begun into the family circumstances of Kumanjayi Little Baby leading up to her disappearance and death, which relatives hope will find answers.

item.title: State government admits police officer battered Hannah Thomas during pro-Palestinian protest
item.description: Lawyers for the state government have admitted police "battered" former Greens candidate Hannah Thomas at a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney last year.

item.title: Hunt for suspects after 12 killed in mass shooting near Johannesburg
item.description: Police launch a manhunt for more than 10 suspects who launched a late-night attack at the Jumpers informal settlement in the Johannesburg suburb of Cleveland.

item.title: Saltwater crocodile injures man at Barred Creek, north of Broome
item.description: Authorities have confirmed the 35-year-old man was fishing in waist deep water at Barred Creek, north of Broome, when he stood on the crocodile.

item.title: Socialite Amy Tossoun faces court over alleged Mount Buller drink spiking
item.description: Melbourne socialite Amy Tossoun has faced a Victorian magistrates' court charged with allegedly spiking a drink at the Mount Buller ski resort.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Seven people and two companies charged after deadly Hong Kong fire
item.description: The seven people charged included directors and inspectors of a consultancy firm involved in the estate's renovation.

item.title: Adelaide woman charged with alleged plot to defraud NDIS of $5 million
item.description: Australian Federal Police charge a 48-year-old Adelaide woman over an alleged plot to steal $5 million from the National Disability Insurance Service and a disability services provider.

item.title: Dog swept out to sea rescued by tour boat off English coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dog rescued off English coast after being blown out to sea in inflatable kayak
item.description: Bruce, a dog, has been rescued from the North Sea after drifting away in an inflatable kayak.

item.title: Coroner releases findings into death of Rosemarie Campbell after gastric bypass
item.description: A woman who died days after gastric bypass surgery likely would have survived if she had not been discharged, an inquest has found.

item.title: Mollie O'Callaghan misses out on Ariarne Titmus's 200m world record at Australian Swimming Trials
item.description: Mollie O'Callaghan shoots out of the gates in the 200m freestyle but cannot maintain the world record pace as she books her ticket to the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

item.title: Belfast stabbing suspect Hadi Alodid in court as victim Stephen Ogilvie loses eye
item.description: Hadi Alodid, a 30-year-old man from Sudan, appears in a Belfast court charged with attempted murder over a stabbing attack that triggered anti-immigrant violence in several parts of Northern Ireland.

item.title: Hundreds protest as One Nation leader Pauline Hanson greets supporters at Perth event
item.description: A crowd of about 400 protesters demonstrates against One Nation's Pauline Hanson as she speaks at a sundowner event in Perth's eastern suburbs.

item.title: Iran says US strikes render ceasefire 'meaningless' – as it happened
item.description: Iran's new body overseeing the Strait of Hormuz has confirmed a complete closure order for the strategic waterway until further notice. Read how events unfolded.

item.title: England drops Stokes from second NZ Test amid 'ongoing investigation'
item.description: The decision to leave both the English captain and bowler Gus Atkinson off the side comes after a reported nightclub incident following the side's win in the first Test.

item.title: Bill Gates 'deeply sorry' for Epstein ties in testimony to US politicians
item.description: The Microsoft co-founder appeared before a closed-door US House Oversight Committee panel that has been investigating the late sex offender's connections with the rich and powerful.

item.title: In Moldova, I meet a woman with a dangerous plan to save her life
item.description: On a park bench in Moldova, I meet a Ukrainian refugee who is planning to return home through a war zone for cancer treatment. We talk for hours.

item.title: Quiz: Do you know where these famous Australian books are set?
item.description: Occupy yourself with some armchair travel courtesy of a selection of our nation's most esteemed literary works.

item.title: Steven Spielberg returns to sci-fi and alien wonder in his thrilling Disclosure Day
item.description: ET director Steven Spielberg dives back into the extra terrestrial with his latest film while asking 'How do you astonish an audience when anything is possible, and nothing is real?

item.title: How the Belfast attack played into the hands of Britain's far right
item.description: As UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer fights for his political life, the country is bracing for what could be a yet another summer of unrest.

item.title: Many households want to electrify. But who pays when the gas utility walks away?
item.description: For many, getting off gas and electrifying their homes is a personal choice. But what happens when an entire town is abandoned by a gas company?

item.title: Albanese's frustration with Trump is on display as he pays a price for an unpopular war
item.description: The Albanese government is paying an economic and political toll for an ongoing and deeply unpopular war.

item.title: Gout Gout sixth in Diamond League debut as Olympic champ Letsile Tebogo claims victory
item.description: Gout Gout finishes well back in the field in the men's 200 metres at the Oslo Diamond League meet, with Olympic champion Letsile Tebogo first across the line.

item.title: Solariums operating underground despite decade-long commercial ban
item.description: Illegal solariums are operating across the country, from single machines secretly set up in suburban neighbourhood garages to those hidden in the back of legitimate businesses.

item.title: Victorian town fights against high suicide rates with community-led prevention training
item.description: After losing a family member to suicide, Peter Taylor vowed to bring mental health first aid to his community.

item.title: Tasmanian Senator Wendy Askew resigns with fellow Liberals, One Nation eyeing seat
item.description: A Tasmanian senator will not recontest her seat in 2028, meaning it is up for the taking by Liberal colleagues and possibly a resurgent One Nation.

item.title: World's largest whale graveyard found in the Indian Ocean
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Residents of new outer-city developments make plea for better public transport in south-east Queensland
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of new homes are being built on the outskirts of greater Brisbane. But residents are concerned if one thing doesn't improve, their quality of life will go backwards.

item.title: As Elon Musk's SpaceX goes public, Australian government officials are flagging Starlink's risks
item.description: As tens of thousands of Australians line up to buy shares in Elon Musk's SpaceX blockbuster public offering, Australian government officials are privately flagging the risks posed by the country's growing reliance on its satellite internet service, Starlink.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: A peculiar peace prize, sky-high tickets and threatened boycotts
item.description: Struggled to keep up with the rollercoaster of controversies that plagued the build-up to this World Cup? We've got you covered.

item.title: Counterfeit cash increase in SA hurting small businesses
item.description: An Adelaide business owner who has been targeted by counterfeiters about a dozen times is warning others to be vigilant, as SA Police warn the number of incidents has increased in the state in the past year.

item.title: Extra maternal and child health nurse visits for children in Victoria under Thriving Kids program
item.description: All children in Victoria will get two extra visits with maternal and child health nurses as the state prepares to launch its new program for those shifted off the NDIS.

item.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with introduction of AI-based language models
item.description: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

item.title: Four men charged after drive-by slingshot attacks on strangers across Brisbane
item.description: Police have charged four men after a series of alleged drive-by attacks using handmade slingshots on members of the public across Brisbane's western suburbs.

item.title: Road trauma victims call for 'overhaul' of Transport Accident Commission in parliamentary inquiry
item.description: A road accident survivor says the organisation that helped her defy all expectations has become a nightmare to deal with.

item.title: The ACT budget quantifies 'the Trump effect' at $2.3m a day. But homegrown decisions are more costly
item.description: The US President, arguably more than the territory's own Treasurer Chris Steel, is very directly adding to the heavy weight dragging on public finances in the nation's capital. Here's how.

item.title: Commonwealth Bank shareholders go to High Court seeking damages for inflated share prices
item.description: The applicants want compensation for the "inflated value of their shares attributable to the bank's wrongful failure to inform the market".

item.title: Petrol station moguls pay $2 million to creditors after empire collapses, land contaminated
item.description: The directors of Zoya Investments have agreed to a $2 million settlement to end the liquidator's case against them.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico City welcomes World Cup but reality bites
item.description: As Mexicans prepare to host or co-host the FIFA World Cup for a record third time, many locals are frustrated by being locked out of the tournament, while others are more optimistic.

item.title: Sydney psychiatrist Gopi Ilawala allowed to continue practising after sexual assault conviction
item.description: The psychiatrist sexually assaulted a colleague in 2022 but a tribunal has "reprimanded" him and allowed him to continue working in medicine.

item.title: Hanson adviser says One Nation 'stole' donation slogan from Sky News host — as it happened
item.description: One of Pauline Hanson's closest advisers says One Nation "stole" its donation campaign slogan from Sky News host Peta Credlin.

item.title: Australia records six-wicket win over West Indies in final Women's T20 World Cup warm-up match
item.description: Australia goes into the Women's T20 World Cup in a positive frame of mind, after Georgia Voll leads a dominant win over West Indies in their final warm-up match in Cardiff.

item.title: Tasmania's first home buyer stamp duty exemption is ending, leaving some scrambling to settle before June 30
item.description: Tasmania's free stamp duty scheme for first home buyers expires at the end of this month, leaving some in an anxious wait to settle before the cut off.

item.title: Missing Nemo from tropical island highlights threat of illegal poaching to Great Barrier Reef
item.description: The little clownfish known as "Island Nemo" was always there for locals and tourists snorkelling in a tropical bay. But then it disappeared and locals suspect foul play.

item.title: Dark Mofo festival begins today in Hobart, including iconic Winter Feast banquet
item.description: Tasmania's two-week Dark Mofo festival kicks off today in Hobart. Popular events such as Winter Feast, Night Mass, the Nude Solstice Swim and the Ogoh Ogoh procession return, while one of the new Spirit of Tasmania ferries will host a free art exhibition.

item.title: Socceroos prepare for the FIFA World Cup
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Grandfather's close call inspires Sunshine Coast student's smart clock invention
item.description: After it took nearly two days for his grandfather to be found following a serious fall, Kai Gould has created a device to connect older people living independently with their loved ones.

item.title: Warning oil could surge to $US150 a barrel with inventories 'critically low' — as it happened
item.description: A major Australian bank has warned the price of oil is at risk of surging to $US150 per barrel as oil inventories approach critically low levels.

item.title: Havana Rose Liu and Leo Woodall on working with legendary Dustin Hoffman
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Towns along 400km of WA coastline battle extreme erosion from winter storms
item.description: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

item.title: Author Maggie O'Farrell on how learning her family history informed her new book
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pope Leo blesses Barcelona's Sagrada Familia as an 'architectural masterpiece'
item.description: The pontiff has celebrated Spain's Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light," as he marked the centenary of the death of its architect Antoni Gaudí.

item.title: The NT government says its tough-on-crime policies are working, despite a string of tragedies and unrest in bush communities.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NT attorney-general says bail reform is working despite string of tragedies
item.description: The government says bail law changes made a year ago are working despite a string of tragedies and unrest in bush communities.

item.title: Donald Trump says US secretly helped ships move oil through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US president says his military has been running a "secret mission" to help tankers and commercial ships defy Iran's blockade.

item.title: Notorious Sydney gang rapist Mohammed Skaf facing drug charges
item.description: NSW Police arrested the 42-year-old in Greenacre in Sydney's south-west overnight and he has been charged with 24 offences.

item.title: Palestinian Australian driver Yasser Shahin prepared for the 24 Hours of Le Mans
item.description: The 24 Hours of Le Mans is a jewel in the motorsport crown, a bucket-list item for drivers and fans, and the 94th edition of the famous race will be held this weekend.

item.title: Stabbing victim's family calls for calm amid violent anti-immigration protests in Northern Ireland
item.description: The family of a Northern Irish man who lost an eye in a knife attack has appealed for calm ​after anti-immigrant violence in Belfast.

item.title: Scientists discover 149 new marine species off Christmas and Cocos (Keeling) Islands
item.description: Researchers have catalogued dozens of new marine species found in the depths of Australia's most remote waters. The result is a window into a rarely glimpsed world.

item.title: UK Police unleash water cannons at protesters in Belfast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Opposition leader Angus Taylor rules out carving up seats with One Nation as party raises $2m
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the Coalition has no plan to share the seats the party targets at the next federal election with One Nation as Pauline Hanson's party says it has raised $2 million since launch.

item.title: The $12 Billion World Cup: Inside FIFA’s Biggest Gamble
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: South Australia secures AFL Gather Round hosting rights until 2029
item.description: The AFL's Gather Round will stay in South Australia until 2029 under a new three-year partnership. It will include a parade and a "feature match" but no decision has been made on venues.

item.title: Trump warns US will 'be attacking' Iran 'very hard'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Solomon Islands won't release security pact with China because of non-disclosure clause
item.description: Prime Minister Matthew Wale says it is legally impossible to publish the final document because the Sogavare government made binding commitments to the Chinese government when it signed the agreement in 2022.

item.title: Mount Isa's pitch for Flying Whales inland airship port uncertain as talks 'stall'
item.description: Giant cargo airships based in outback Queensland are being pitched as a freight solution for remote Australia, but questions remain about how the project will go ahead.

item.title: Three hospitalised after family dispute at Oak Valley outstation turns violent
item.description: Three people have been hospitalised after a family dispute at a Central Australian outstation turned violent over the weekend, sparking fears of further unrest.

item.title: Gout Gout would be wise to take heed of Letsile Tebogo's advice after Diamond League debut
item.description: Gout Gout can benefit from Letsile Tebogo's wisdom, as the Olympic champion knows what it is like to make the challenging transition from junior star to the senior ranks.

item.title: Culture in Australia's largest police force 'unacceptable'
item.description: Bullying, discrimination and sexual harassment are occurring at "unacceptable levels" within the NSW Police Force and employees are too afraid to speak up, an independent report has found.

item.title: Albany musician Eddy Jay turns to 3D printer for new supply of musical instruments
item.description: After a music career that's taken him around the world, Eddy Jay has turned to cutting-edge manufacturing to make his own instruments.

item.title: Queensland hospice's end-of-life service kept alive after 'generous' donations
item.description: A regional Queensland palliative care facility has been given a new lease on life after receiving two major donations from businesses in Hervey Bay.

item.title: Residents evacuated after protesters set fire to houses in Belfast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Musician prints his own instruments
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Launceston City Council receives $100k in insurance after trophy, featuring 24-carat gold sheep, stolen
item.description: The trophy features a sculpture that includes a 24-carat gold merino sheep and a weaving loom made of silver and platinum, with its theft last year captured on CCTV.

item.title: SA consumer watchdog says real estate agents can be liable if aware of property issues
item.description: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.

item.title: Confirmed Ebola virus cases near 600 as outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo grows
item.description: In just two weeks the number of confirmed Ebola virus cases has increased more than eightfold, with at least 100 people now dead.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos star Mo Toure misses training days out from opening game against Türkiye
item.description: Socceroos striker Mo Toure is a no-show at the beginning of a scheduled training session, bringing his fitness into doubt just days out from their World Cup opener against Türkiye.

item.title: ACT Education Minister Yvette Berry censured in Legislative Assembly as teachers go on strike
item.description: ACT MLA Yvette Berry is censured in the Legislative Assembly over her record as minister for housing and education amid a teachers' strike that has closed schools across the territory.

item.title: Families demand police accountability as pain from decades-old cold cases still raw
item.description: The first hearing of a NSW inquiry into missing persons has heard from the family of a teenage girl who went missing in the 1970s near where notorious killer Ivan Milat was operating.

item.title: Girlfriend of man shot by police disputes that officers told him to drop knife
item.description: A coronial inquest into the shooting has heard from the woman who was in a locked bathroom with her armed partner for hours of police negotiations.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kick-off times in Australian time, when to watch in Australia
item.description: Check out when your team's matches are kicking off at the 2026 FIFA World Cup so you don't miss a match.

item.title: State and territory disability ministers lash federal government's NDIS changes
item.description: State and territory disability ministers have accused the Albanese government of pushing on with NDIS reform that does not align with previous agreements.

item.title: Teenage boy pleads guilty to armed rape of woman during Cairns home invasion
item.description: A court heard the boy, now 16, was armed with a tomahawk when he broke into a couple's Cairns home with two other teenagers in February last year.

item.title: World Cup: FIFA chief Gianni Infantino dismisses concerns over ticketing, visa issues
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino defends soccer's governing body's handling of visa issues and ticket prices ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: Coronial probe into Melbourne aged care COVID outbreak resumes after years-long hiatus
item.description: An inquest examining 50 COVID-era deaths at a Melbourne aged care home has resumed after a years-long hiatus.

item.title: New York Knicks complete record NBA Finals comeback against San Antonio Spurs
item.description: The New York Knicks complete the greatest comeback in NBA Finals history, rallying from 29 points down to beat San Antonio in game four and move to the brink of a first title in 53 years.

item.title: Man accused of forging estranged wife's death certificate prohibited from selling shared properties
item.description: The estranged husband of Rehana Parvin, whose body was found in Bangladesh in 2024, will not be allowed to sell the three homes they owned together.

item.title: Adelaide men jailed for $6 million fraudulent property investment scheme
item.description: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

item.title: Casey McLean's arrival to State of Origin is proof the future is now
item.description: By the first month of his 20s, Casey McLean had already achieved more than most players do in a lifetime but ahead of his first run-on Origin match the first of a new generation of stars is still just getting started.

item.title: Former police officer who harassed woman online sentenced to six months' prison
item.description: A former Victoria Police officer is sentenced to six months in prison after repeatedly harassing a woman on social media.

item.title: Daylesford pub did not hold valid outdoor dining permit at time of deadly crash, court hears
item.description: A Victorian court hears that a regional pub where five people died when a driver had a hypoglycaemic episode, crashing his car through diners, did not have a valid outdoor dining permit at the time of the crash.

item.title: Former bikie boss Angelo Pandeli arrested in Bali using fake Brazilian passport
item.description: Former bikie boss Angelo Pandeli was attempting to fly to Africa on a fake Brazilian passport when Indonesian immigration authorities apprehended him over an Interpol notice.

item.title: Anti-immigration figures seizing on outrage at Northern Ireland stabbing to drive people towards cause
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk and far-right activist Tommy Robinson have seized upon the alleged stabbing of a man in Belfast by a Sudanese immigrant to push an anti-immigration agenda.

item.title: RecipeTin Eats founder Nagi adopts new canine companion
item.description: Nagi Maehashi, the founder of RecipeTin Eats, introduces her new canine companion Jaffle.

item.title: FIFA president on Iran's participation in 2026 World Cup
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Alleged arsonist Jonathan Rinderknecht lonely, dejected when he lit Palisades fire, court hears
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht's attorney countered that a hilltop blaze was triggered by fireworks, not his client, and that the massive Palisades inferno that killed 12 people was a separate fire set by others.

item.title: Dictor Dongrin's death in custody subject of critical findings by NSW coroner
item.description: A NSW coroner recommends conduct reviews for medical staff over their treatment of a prisoner who died within a day of entering Australia's largest prison.

item.title: Andrew Mungatopi sentenced to 10 years in jail for stabbing death of Tiwi Islands sistergirl
item.description: Andrew Mungatopi has been sentenced to 10 years and two months in prison for the fatal stabbing of his niece in a Darwin backyard in 2024.

item.title: Emotional Hanson blames Abbott for electoral fraud 'witch hunt'
item.description: As Anthony Albanese stood in front of a Medicare banner and Pauline Hanson put her hand out for donations, voters could be forgiven for thinking the election was two months, not two years, away.

item.title: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff links Madeleine Ogilvie's court action to Integrity Commission
item.description: Premier Jeremy Rockliff links comments made by the boss of the state's Integrity Commission to a question about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's Supreme Court action.

item.title: Former Labor ministers accused of political interference at Queensland CFMEU inquiry
item.description: The probe has wrapped up its tenth block of public hearings with explosive new allegations of political interference levelled against senior Palaszczuk government ministers Mick de Brenni and Grace Grace.

item.title: Adelaide lawyer Andrew Graham pleads not guilty to sexual offences
item.description: Andrew Graham enters not guilty pleas to two counts of rape and one charge of compelling a person to sexually manipulate an offender.

item.title: BHP facing 'historic' strike as union members back industrial action in Port Hedland
item.description: Unionised workers at BHPs operations in Port Hedland could walk off the job as soon as next week after endorsing protected industrial action.

item.title: Pauline Hanson emotional as she speaks on brief stint in jail
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson says she takes policy advice from 'friend' Gina Rinehart
item.description: Pauline Hanson speaks of her "respect" for billionaire Gina Rinehart , becoming emotional during a speech recalling her time in detention.

item.title: Are YouTubers taking over the film industry?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Inspirational' Dawson stars as Crows send premiership statement
item.description: Jordan Dawson plays one of the best quarters of his career as Adelaide claims a 57-point win over the Western Bulldogs at Docklands.

item.title: 'Trying not to cry': Rabbitohs rise after emotional Jai Arrow moment
item.description: Jai Arrow has inspired South Sydney to a commanding win over the Brisbane Broncos, on an emotional night at Stadium Australia. Follow live.

item.title: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
item.description: The men were accused of carrying out the attack on the popular Erawan Shrine that killed 20 people, many of whom were Chinese tourists.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Rosemarie Campbell's family calls for accountability after coroner finds death was preventable
item.description: The family of a Gold Coast woman who died following complications from a surgery performed by Reza Adib have called for accountability and meaningful change in the wake of damning inquest findings.

item.title: Byron Bay Bluesfest liquidator’s report reveals creditors owed $7 million
item.description: A report into the cancelled Byron Bay Bluesfest reveals creditors are unlikely to be repaid the millions of dollars they are owed.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns' Max Knobel suspended for four AFL/VFL games over homophobic slur
item.description: Gold Coast Suns ruck Max Knobel is banned over a homophobic slur during a VFL game, meaning he will be unavailable until after round 17 of the AFL season.

item.title: Australians face a 'binary choice' as One Nation soars, says Jim Chalmers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers hits out at Pauline Hanson's ties to Gina Rinehart and questions whether she actually stands for battling Australians.

item.title: Man, 58, charged with allegedly harassing Bondi royal commission witness
item.description: The man from northern NSW was also found in possession of neo-Nazi material during a search warrant on Thursday.

item.title: Queensland youth detention staff walk off job, children locked down for entire weekend
item.description: Children incarcerated at two south-east Queensland youth detention facilities were allegedly locked down for an entire weekend due to staff shortages that have prompted dozens of workers across the state to walk off the job.

item.title: Explosive allegations heard at Queensland CFMEU inquiry
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: How the major political parties are planning to take on One Nation
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Kaylee McKeown still waiting for new personal best, world record after Australian Swimming Trials
item.description: Backstroke superstar Kaylee McKeown leaves the Australian Swimming Trials frustrated as her own world record remains just out of reach.

item.title: Police escort then-suspect Bangkok bombers outside court in 2015.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Richard Marles 'stood up' on AUKUS trip after British counterpart John Healey's shock resignation
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles's visit to the United Kingdom has been thrown into chaos, after his British counterpart John Healey resigned hours before they were due to speak together.

item.title: Six legendary Australian music acts inducted into ARIA Hall of Fame after 'a pretty amazing journey'
item.description: Gurrumul, Jenny Morris, Kate Ceberano, Spiderbait, The Living End, and Vika and Linda Bull were elevated six to top-tier status at a ceremony in Sydney as part of ARIA's 40th anniversary celebrations.

item.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway
item.description: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

item.title: Live: Trump cancels US strikes on Iran after threatening 'very hard' attack
item.description: Both sides have launches fresh strikes in the Gulf this week as fears mount the near three-month conflict cannot be brought to a swift end.

item.title: Live: World Cup 2026 gets underway at the Azteca
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup gets underway at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City, with the opening ceremony followed by the hosts, Mexico taking on South Africa in the opening game. Follow live.
